utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists five items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five items. Each one contains the relevant coverage details and associated costs (monthly or total breakdown). Specifically: health_coverage provides both physical and mental coverage with costs; property_coverage includes home/contents coverage with costs; travel_coverage is present with travel-related coverage and costs; additional_services lists enhancement services with a setup fee; and total_budget provides a complete cost breakdown. All required details are fully specified.

To map this to the ground-truth ratings, we must deconstruct each coverage type and match it to the closest equivalent options in the ground-truth dataset. The health_coverage includes both physical and mental health coverage, reflecting a balanced premium family plan. The closest matches for top-tier combined coverage are 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' or 'EliteCare Premium Plan', both rated 10 in both physical and mental health categories. The travel_coverage includes comprehensive features such as medical evacuation and business travel protection, matching 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' or 'EliteTravel Premium Plan', rated 10. The additional services section (concierge_services, dedicated_agent, priority_claims, direct_billing, etc.) aligns with 'Premium Insurance Membership' (rating 10) and 'Insurance Portfolio Management' (rating 9) from general_services. Therefore, the final mapping will include these items and their ratings from the ground-truth data.
